claim: Women with a higher birth weight are more likely to develop breast cancer later in life.
evidence_sentences: RESULTS We found that heavier birth weights were associated with increased breast cancer risk, with studies involving five categories of birth weight identifying odds ratios (ORs) of 1.24 (95% confidence interval [CI] 1.04 to 1.48) for 4,000 g or more and 1.15 (95% CI 1.04 to 1.26) for 3,500 g to 3,999 g, relative to a birth weight of 2,500 to 2,599 g. These studies provided no support for a J-shaped relationship of birthweight to risk.

conditions: birth weight ≥4000 g vs 2500–2599 g reference
birth weight 3500–3999 g vs 2500–2599 g reference
